--- title: "Staff Absence Intelligence" format: dashboard: theme: flatly embed-resources: true keep-ipynb: false execute: echo: false warning: false jupyter: python3 --- # Executive Overview ## Row {height=25%} ::: {#8ae14ca4 .cell component='valuebox' execution_count=2} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` {'value': '1,000', 'header': 'Total Staff Analyzed', 'icon': 'people', 'color': 'light'} ``` ::: ::: ::: {#ebec44bb .cell component='valuebox' execution_count=3} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` {'value': '76.3%', 'header': "Forecast Accuracy Improvement", 'icon': 'graph-up', 'color': 'primary'} ``` ::: ::: ::: {#45693baf .cell component='valuebox' execution_count=4} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ``` {'value': '0.60', 'header': "Duration Predictive Power", 'icon': 'bullseye', 'color': 'info'} ``` ::: ::: ## Row ### Strategic Summary This page provides a high-level summary of the key findings from the sickness absence analysis. Use this space to outline strategic recommendations for the wellbeing team, informed by the detailed analysis on the other pages. Based on the analysis of **1,000** staff records, we have identified key drivers of sickness absence and built predictive models to support proactive wellbeing strategies. #### Key Findings * **Forecasting Absences:** The time-series model predicts daily absence volumes with an average error of **14.7** days (MAE). This gives the resource planning team highly reliable estimates of future staffing gaps. * **Predicting Return to Work:** The survival model predicts the likelihood of staff returning to work with a score of **0.60** (Concordance Score). * **Risk Factors (Prolonging Absence):** The analysis shows that **Age** significantly prolongs the duration of sickness absences. **Recommendation:** Implement targeted wellbeing check-ins for these demographics early in their absence. * **Protective Factors (Shortening Absence):** Conversely, **Imd Quintile**, **Gender M**, **Role Manager**, **Role Admin** are associated with significantly shorter absence durations. # CPH Model Outputs ## Row ### Model Accuracy (Concordance Index) **Concordance Score:** 0.599 This metric evaluates the model's predictive accuracy by measuring how well it ranks the order of events—specifically, predicting who will return to work first. The score ranges from **0.5** (equivalent to random guessing) to **1.0** (perfect prediction). ## Row {height=400px} ### How to Interpret the Model {width = 40%} This table translates the statistical model into practical insights, highlighting the factors that significantly impact how quickly an employee returns to work. For **continuous variables** (like Age): The percentage represents the change in the likelihood of returning to work on any given day for every 1-unit increase in the variable. For example, if age decreases the likelihood by 1.9%, it means for every 1 year older an employee is, their likelihood of returning to work on any given day reduces by 1.9%. For **categorical variables** (like Role or Gender): The percentage represents the change in likelihood compared to the baseline group (e.g., being a Manager vs. the default role). ### Hazard Ratios Plain English Interpretation {width = 60%} ::: {#c920e4d6 .cell execution_count=7} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=7} ```{=html}
interpretation
Variable
age Decreases the likelihood of returning to work by 1.9% (PROLONGS absence)
imd_quintile Increases the likelihood of returning to work by 12.7% (SHORTENS absence)
gender_M Increases the likelihood of returning to work by 27.4% (SHORTENS absence)
role_Manager Increases the likelihood of returning to work by 28.1% (SHORTENS absence)
role_Admin Increases the likelihood of returning to work by 49.7% (SHORTENS absence)
``` ::: ::: ## Column {width = 100%} ### Probability of Remaining Off-work {.panel-tabset} ### Age ```{=html}

``` # Time Series Forecast ## Row {height = 70%} ### Force-wide Absence Forecast This chart compares the performance of a sophisticated SARIMA model against a simple Seasonal Naive forecast. The SARIMA model provides a more accurate prediction by capturing complex trends and seasonality in the historical absence data. ```{=html}
``` ## Row {height = 30%} ### Model Performance and Interpretation **Forecasting Accuracy (Mean Absolute Error):** The model is currently able to predict the absence rate with an average error of **14.7**. This is **76.3% more accurate** than simply assuming the absence rate will be exactly the same as it was at this time last year (the baseline 'Seasonal Naive' method, which has an average error of **62.1**). > *Note: A 'Concordance Score' is used to measure the accuracy of Time-to-Event (Survival) models. For time series forecasting like this chart, we use Mean Absolute Error (MAE), which tells us exactly how much our forecast is off by on average.*
